description: Round-trip vectors for POST /v1-verify-permit. Response is value-based fail-closed: the SDK MUST treat `valid` and `outcome` in the body as authoritative, regardless of HTTP status.

vectors.name: verify_permit_happy
vectors.description: Happy-path verification with actor_id + action_type binding.

vectors.name: verify_permit_minimum_payload
vectors.description: Verification with only the permit_token. actor_id / action_type / traceparent MUST be omitted from the wire, not serialized as empty strings.

vectors.name: verify_permit_already_used
vectors.description: Single-use replay. HTTP 200, but valid=false. SDK MUST NOT raise from the transport layer; it MUST return the envelope for the caller to inspect.

wire_response.reason: This permit token has already been consumed

sdk_output.reason: This permit token has already been consumed

vectors.name: verify_permit_actor_mismatch
vectors.description: Binding check failure. The SDK MUST forward actor_id / action_type cross-check fields so the server can detect replays across actors.

wire_response.reason: actor_id does not match the original evaluation

sdk_output.reason: actor_id does not match the original evaluation

vectors.name: verify_permit_4xx_still_value_based
vectors.description: Server may return 403 with the same value-based envelope (e.g. INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE). SDK MUST parse the body, not raise based on HTTP status.

vectors.name: verify_response_missing_valid
vectors.description: Malformed response: missing `valid`. SDKs MUST raise a transport-unavailable error.
